# arubasdwan_security_policy (Resource)

Manages a security policy rule in the Aruba SD-WAN Orchestrator. Uses the /vrf/config/securityPolicies API endpoints.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "arubasdwan_security_policy" "example" {
  segment_pair   = "0:0"
  source_zone_id = 1
  dest_zone_id   = 2
  priority       = 30000
  action         = "allow"
  app_group      = "WebApps"
  comment        = "Allow web apps"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `action` (String) The action to take: "allow" or "deny".
- `dest_zone_id` (Number) The destination security zone ID.
- `priority` (Number) The rule priority (20000-65535, lower number = higher priority).
- `segment_pair` (String) The segment pair identifier (e.g. "0_0").
- `source_zone_id` (Number) The source security zone ID.

### Optional

- `acl` (String) ACL class name to match.
- `app_group` (String) Application group to match.
- `application` (String) Application name to match.
- `comment` (String) A comment for the policy rule.
- `dscp` (String) DSCP value to match.
- `dst_address_group` (String) Destination IP address group to match. Reference an arubasdwan_ip_address_group by name.
- `dst_dns` (String) Destination DNS/domain pattern to match.
- `dst_geo` (String) Destination geo location to match.
- `dst_ip` (String) Destination IP address or range to match.
- `dst_port` (String) Destination port or range to match.
- `dst_service` (String) Destination service to match.
- `either_address_group` (String) Match either direction against an IP address group. Reference an arubasdwan_ip_address_group by name.
- `either_dns` (String) Either DNS/domain pattern to match (supports wildcards, e.g. "*google.com").
- `either_geo` (String) Either geo location to match.
- `either_ip` (String) Either source or destination IP to match (mutually exclusive with src_ip/dst_ip).
- `either_port` (String) Either source or destination port to match.
- `either_service` (String) Either service to match.
- `log_priority` (String) The syslog priority level ("0"–"7"). If omitted, the existing Orchestrator value is preserved (default for new rules: "0"). Must be set explicitly when logging is "enable".
- `logging` (String) Whether logging is enabled for this rule: "enable" or "disable". If omitted, the existing Orchestrator value is preserved (default for new rules: "disable"). When set to "enable", log_priority must also be explicitly set in the configuration.
- `overlay` (String) Overlay to match.
- `protocol` (String) Protocol to match (e.g. "tcp", "udp", "ip").
- `rule_state` (String) Whether the rule is enabled: "enable" or "disable". If omitted, the existing Orchestrator value is preserved (default for new rules: "enable").
- `src_address_group` (String) Source IP address group to match. Reference an arubasdwan_ip_address_group by name.
- `src_dns` (String) Source DNS/domain pattern to match.
- `src_geo` (String) Source geo location to match (e.g. "US", "DE").
- `src_ip` (String) Source IP address or range to match.
- `src_port` (String) Source port or range to match.
- `src_service` (String) Source service (SaaS app name or organization) to match.
- `vlan` (String) Interface/VLAN to match (e.g. "lan0").

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Composite identifier: segment_pair/src_dst/priority.
